entry.title: SAP QIM Process Mapping in SAP BTP Feasibility
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Since the supporting is ending for SAP QIM do we have any other alternatives ? Is there a way to handle SAP QIM things into SAP BTP.

Thanks,

Rahul

entry.title: Link Asset to Project/WBS Element or Maintenance Order
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My customer currently has orders of order type "Standing order" (PM) in the legacy system (SAP ERP 6), on which all costs associated with a certain fixed asset are collected and settled.

This is to be mapped in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. A maintenance order is not necessarily required, as no logistical processes need to be recorded, but only normal costs (invoice, time confirmations, activity allocation, etc.). Projects/WBS elements would even be preferred.

However, it is important to the customer that a fixed asset can be assigned in some way so that the customer knows to which asset the costs are posted. The fixed asset is not the recipient of settlements, but only has an informational character.

In the legacy system, this asset can be assigned via the "Location" tab in the order. From the asset master record, you can access the standing order via Environment->Equipment. In the equipment master, the standing order is then assigned in the "Organization" tab. This gives the customer a connection in both directions.

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, as far as I know, it is no longer possible to create a connection between a fixed asset and an equipment. I also see no other way to assign an order or a WBS element in the asset master record, except for a WBS element as an account assignment element.

Furthermore, I cannot find the possibility to assign an asset directly, neither in a maintenance order nor in a project/WBS element.

Does anyone have experience in a similar topic? Is there possibly a scope item with a functionality that could fulfil these requirements?

Thank you in advance for any tips!

Best regards
Rado

entry.title: Re: Production Order – Planned Status Not Visible in late...
entry.content:
Hi All,

After a long days,I got a response from SAP.This is the final conclusion of this issue

SAP words:

"Please note that the expected system behavior is that the system does NOT allow you to change the status of the Production Order with existing transactions back to Planned.

Unfortunately the previous behavior was not desired one. The reason is that In certain scenarios it was resulting in data corruption.

We therefore do not have any way to bring back to old and incorrect behavior".

entry.title: Re: Now available: ABAP Platform Trial
entry.content:
SAP GUI Download should be available for everyone using this link:

https://developers.sap.com/trials-downloads.html?search=sap+gui

Thanks for providing the image. It is 7.54, by the way, and lacks some CDS functionality, such as define entity. That starts with 7.55. Hopefully, in the future, someone will have the time and resources to provide an updated image.

entry.title: Re: Now available: ABAP Platform Trial
entry.content:
Hi pokrakam!

2022 is almost finished, due to unforeseen technical reasons, give us a few more weeks.

SP01 is not a typo. SP07 is the support package of the ABAP Platform 1909.  Because the trial is been covered by an own (new) development program, it starts with it's own versioning. SP0... SP1

You might get SAPGui here

https://developers.sap.com/trials-downloads.html?search=ABAP+Platform+1909+Developer+Edition+on+SAP+HANA+2.0

entry.title: Re: Journal Entry - Change (Asynchronous) to update CD am...
entry.content:
Hello JimE, thank you for your response.

I would like to share some additional comments on this topic.

  • SAP has a Journal Entry - Update Discount Information API that enables modifications to the CD Amount field. However, it has certain constraints, such as the lack of support for bulk updates, and it cannot process inbound messages if no corresponding outbound message has been sent previously. For more information on outbound messages. Would you like to use this as a reference and officially add the CD Amount field in the Journal Entry - Change API?
  • When integrating with external systems and using percentage fields, decimal rounding issues arise due to the 3-decimal limitation. Therefore, we have experienced stakeholders sometimes asking for CD Amount to update operations.
  • CD Amount does not even exist in the Journal Entry - Post

entry.title: Re: Journal Entry - Change (Asynchronous) to update CD am...
entry.content: Looking forward to the response on above comments

entry.title: Re: SAC Cross-Model Parameter for Date
entry.content: and https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/283996 😉

entry.title: PO Release strategy based on cost centers
entry.content:
Hello everybody! A customer needs  a new release strategy based on cost centers and I know that it cannot be done without ABAP. The PO release strategy is based on CEKKO structure and we don't have KOSTL field included. Any advice/suggestion is welcome. You can find the scenario below. 

Thank you in advance!

 

< 5,000 EURLevel 1     
> 5,000 EURLevel 1 + Level 2 + Level 3 (+ Level 4, as appropriate)     
       
       
OwnerCost CenterCost Center NameLevel 1Level 2Level 3Level 4
X  PMFDGM 
 TBUGA99003General Supply Chain Expenses    
 TBUIM10911IM Bhuler-Line    
 TBUIM10912IM Bhuler-2    
 TBUIM10921IM Mazzetti 28    
 TBUIM10922IM Mazzetti 22    
 TBUIM10923IM Bindler Line    
 TBUIM10931IM Foil Chocolate    
 TBUIM10999IM Other Choco    
 TBUIM20921IM-Extruder-Solich-Line    
 TBUIM20931IM-Sugus-Line    
 TBUIM20952IM-PACK SUGAR SC    
 TBUIM20962IM-Ernst Syrup    
 TBUIM20999IM Other Sugar    
 TBUIM30911IM-Magura    
 TBUIM30999IM-Other Magura    
 TBUIM40101IM-SC-Plant Mgm    
 TBUIM40201IM-SC-Planning    
 TBUIM40401IM-SC-Warehouse RM    
 TBUIM40501IM-SC-Warehouse FG Choco    
 TBUIM40502IM-SC-Warehouse FG Sugar    
       
   TMSCDFDGM
 TBUIM40601IM-SC-Engineers    
 TBUIM40602IM-SC-Metrology    
 TBUIM40603IM-SC-Electricians    
 TBUIM40604IM-SC-Central work-shop    
 TBUIM40605IM-SC-WWT    
 TBUIM40701IM-SC-Safety    
       
       
YTBULE11001CU-LegalGCFDGM 
       
Z  SDTTFDGM 
 TBUSR10101CU-Traditional Trade     
 TBUSR10104CU-Own shop    
       
W  SDMTFDGM 
 TBUSR10102CU-Modern Trade    
 TBUSR10103CU-Trade Marketing

entry.title: Re: Handling Unit Label Printing Through PPF in SAP EWM
entry.content: Hi, will explore this, thank you.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Paris
entry.content:
Brought to you by SAP Community members, the SAP Stammtisch is an informal meetup everyone can participate: SAP users, developers, consultants and architects - come and join us and bring a friend. 

This special occurrence invites special guests: @walid kindly invited the Developer Advocates Rich Heilman and Thomas Jung, who are hosting an SAP CodeJam on RAP & CAP the day after (April 19).

Leave your RSVP for the SAP Stammtisch here or register via the official registration page (in French only).

Did you know there is a specific Paris Group? Join the group and connect with other local peers!

entry.title: Re: Consume a Parameterized View from External OData v2 s...
entry.content: You need to define an entity with parameters in CAP. You can use the types from the import in this definition. But declaring the parameters and results as independent entities did not work in my experiments; there are subtle metadata aspects/relations that are generated on the fly for entities with parameters.

entry.title: CCV2 (SAP Commerce Cloud) mTLS (Mutual TLS, Two-Way TLS) Support?
entry.content:
Does SAP Commerce Cloud's web service layer allow for Mutual TLS (mTLS, Two-Way TLS) support? 

I know that SAP Commerce Cloud (CCV2) enables you to upload SSL/TLS certificates for endpoints and allows you to upload Trusted Certificates for scenarios where the backend connects to external services, but I am not sure on the configurability of the SAP Commerce Cloud environment to support configuring the certificates for mTLS.

 

SAP Commerce Cloud

entry.title: Re: Now available: ABAP Platform Trial
entry.content:
Hi @ralf_henning , 

Thanks for clarifying SP01, and excellent news on 2022, I'll wait for that. 

Re. SAP GUI, I should have been more specific: Where can we get a recent SAP GUI. The developer download is 7.8, we are up to 8.0 SP4 I think (I'm running 8.00 and it's as buggy as can be expected from a .00 version)

entry.title: The Future is Now: Reflections on SAP Data Unleashed 2024
entry.content:
The virtual curtains of SAP Data Unleashed 2024 have just closed, leaving us all energized and brimming with inspiration. We all agree that to achieve true digital transformation, enterprises must have seamless access to their business-critical data no matter where it resides across processes, applications, and value tech. After last year’s resounding success, This highly anticipated event delved into the future of data, AI, and planning, offered a glimpse into its transformative potential, and provided tangible strategies that can truly enable our businesses to thrive in an increasingly data-driven world.

As automation revolutionizes the way we live and work, data management and analytics become the defining resource of successful companies. They have quickly become the defining resources for driving innovation, improving decision-making, and enhancing customer experiences. The SAP Data Unleashed 2024 shed the spotlight on the latest innovations and partnerships aimed at empowering organizations to harness the full potential of their data. We're now truly ushering in a new era of data management, analytics, and AI governance that can enable us to derive value and enable data-driven decision-making.

Unified Data Management with SAP Datasphere

One of the most refreshing aspects of SAP Data Unleashed 2024 was the focus on practical applications rather than mere buzzwords. The event served as a platform for SAP, along with its open data partners, to unveil new data and generative AI capabilities and the latest innovations in the powerful open data ecosystem around the SAP Datasphere solutions. 

This business data fabric architecture empowers organizations to connect, unify, and manage their data seamlessly— regardless of source or environment—eliminating the burden of duplication and complexity: the result, is streamlined data processes and invaluable insights driving strategic decision-making. The keynote speaker, Juergen Mueller, CTO and Member of the Executive Board at SAP, presented concrete case studies showcasing how organizations are successfully implementing these concepts to achieve tangible results. 

Furthermore, the sessions delved into the nuances of pairing SAP Datasphere with SAP Analytics Cloud to add that extra zing to informed and data-driven decision-making. By integrating these two platforms, organizations gain the ability to perform cross-organizational planning, conduct data-driven simulations, and leverage generative AI powered by SAP HANA Cloud. This synergy empowers businesses to anticipate market trends, optimize resource allocation, and drive efficiency across their operations.

Hear more about the SAP Data Unleashed 2024 and the future of Data 2024 from John "JG" Chirapurath.

For many of us, SAP Data Unleashed 2024 served as a powerful confirmation of the direction our companies are already heading. The emphasis on actionable insights deeply resonated, as we witnessed real-world case studies showcasing how organizations are integrating AI into ERP systems and leveraging data analytics to gain a deeper understanding of customer behavior. These discussions mirrored our own ongoing efforts and aspirations at Crave InfoTech, providing valuable validation and a sense of shared purpose.

Overcoming Data Fragmentation with SAP Data Unleashed

With Gen AI moving from hype to everyday norm— the future beyond any doubt— belongs to data-driven organizations. Yet many organizations still struggle with disparate data sources, siloed systems, and fragmented insights, hindering their ability to make informed decisions. With SAP Datasphere, businesses can overcome these challenges by seamlessly integrating data from across the enterprise, enabling holistic insights that drive strategic action. Customers leveraging SAP Datasphere have reported significant improvements in decision-making agility, operational efficiency, and competitive advantage. 

A unified approach to data management, analytics, and AI governance can equip future-ready enterprises with unprecedented insights, drive innovation, and achieve sustainable growth. Crave InfoTech’s sustainability solution, Intelligent Ecological Balance Reporting leverages this approach to offer organizational carbon footprints analyzing and reporting capabilities.

Our sustainability solution, built on SAP BTP, offers Intelligent Ecological Balance Reporting and Analysis. It helps organizations set and achieve sustainable goals, assess current conditions, and monitor performance in real-time. Integrating with SAP S/4HANA, it empowers businesses for long-term sustainability with zero emissions, waste, and inequality ambitions. Leveraging SAC and SAP data analytics, the solution provides insights to make informed decisions based on environmental indicators.

With deep expertise in data management, analytics, and gen AI, Crave InfoTech is uniquely positioned to address today's businesses' complex challenges. Our Crave Cloud ALM Intelligent Insights & Automation Suite (CALM) solution offers advanced capabilities for enhanced application lifecycle management, seamlessly integrating with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud. This aligns with our commitment to innovation and delivering value to customers embarking on their data-driven journey.

Looking Forward, Together

SAP Data Unleashed 2024 was the forum for organizations seeking a glimpse into the future of data, AI, and planning. It gave us a great opportunity to gain fresh perspectives, discover actionable strategies, and connect with a forward-thinking community. 

The future is here, and SAP Data Unleashed 2024 has equipped us with the knowledge and connections to navigate it with confidence and purpose. At Crave Infotech, our commitment to innovation, coupled with our proven track record of delivering value to customers, makes us the trusted partner for organizations embarking on their data-driven journey. We encourage other organizations to embrace similar opportunities for continuous learning and collaboration in this exciting era.

Together, we can shape a future where data is not just a commodity, but a catalyst for transformative change. Let's unlock the power of data together.

entry.title: Re: VA02 from an ABAP program
entry.content: @SantoshB do you mean a custom Z field that is displayed on the additional data tab?

entry.title: How to get the most from S/4HANA and Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
Across thousands of enterprises of every size, most users now ‘live’ in Microsoft Teams.  It has become the de facto place for people to communicate and collaborate, and is increasingly the tool to which people turn first in order to get work done.  As a result, it is becoming more and more important for organisations to find ways to bring information and work into Teams: It is sensible to bring work to your employees rather than for them to find it in one or many Inboxes.

SAP and Microsoft have worked together for many years to deliver integrations between SAP’s ERP platforms and Microsoft’s office platform, and the story continues to evolve.

Sharing Fiori content

With S4HANA 2023 came the ability to share content from inside Fiori apps with work colleagues via Teams.  This means that data can be shared instantly into a conversation, driving better collaboration and decision-making.  This requires the installation of the ‘S/4HANA Teams App’ for every employee.

Business Process approvals

SAP has provided infrastructure and development tools to extend approval processes into Teams by cloning SAP Workflow steps into Teams.  This is achieved through the development of a custom application for the particular workflow step, in order to pass workflow and application data into the Teams chat, using a Microsoft Adaptive Card.  The approach requires quite a bit of infrastructure set-up and work in SAP BTP, S/4HANA and Azure.  The data flow looks like:

Chatbot

Another option is to build a chatbot and deploy in Teams to provide process details.  Questions can be defined to which the bot can provide a response, including real-time data from S/4HANA.

You will also find some point solutions for specific business processes to enable some connectivity to Teams.

Platform

Arch provides Looply, an integration platform for enterprise processes and Teams. 

Looply enable you to extend more processes to Teams, with less custom development.  Looply is shipped with an add-on integration to S/4HANA / ECC, a plug-in component which handles the communication to and from the cloud environment.

With Looply you can send process notifications through Teams, as well as action cards for process approval and rejection.  Each participant in the process can be updated when a change is made, not just the approver.  This provides  process transparency and control as well as accelerated processes, as users are able to conduct more work without leaving Teams.

Looply uses Microsoft Adaptive Cards which can be generated using AI.

 


One difference with Looply is that you can configure and deploy your own Teams apps, with your own branding.  Another difference is that there is no reliance on SAP Event Mesh or need to build a custom app in BTP for every integration step. Looply can integrate with SAP Event Mesh if necessary for events triggered from other SAP enterprise solutions.

Summary

Great benefits await organizations through the integration of S/4HANA and Microsoft Teams.  You can transform internal communication and collaboration, improve decision-making and accelerate key business processes.

Some approaches require considerable development, but solutions like Looply are making it easier for more organizations to reap the benefits.

entry.title: Re: How to make Mandatory a Field
entry.content: Yes. you can make a field to mandatory in configuration / enhancement / BADI . Can you be more specific on which module and which screen ?

entry.title: Re: How to make Mandatory a Field

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2023.004 v1.10: novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
Oi @Adri_Ferreira01,

As SAP Notes para SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opções de emissão de fatura de saída e de entrada para o Brasil e SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletrônica) estão planejadas para serem liberadas até o fim de março. 🙂

entry.title: can we extend the text field character limit from 50 to 500 in fbl5n?
entry.content: We have a requirement to extend the character limit in FBL5N text field from 50 to 500. Kindly let us know for any possibilities.

entry.title: Re: What`s new and all about SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration?
entry.content:
Pardha,

We would like to replicate released operation confirmations of workorders into its ERP counterpart. So far, we understand that this feature is not out of the box and we need to create a custom workflow associated to an external event (according to the last release documentation).

However we do not see the External Event for Workorder Confirmation on the Manage Workflow application of the SAP Business Network Collaboration. We only see Equipment, Functional Location and Model events.

Probably I missing something from the documentation in order to activate this function.

Ay comment will be appreciated.

Patricio.-

entry.title: Re: The Value of Learning & SAP SuccessFactors Learning Roadmap - APJ/EMEA Session

entry.title: Re: How to release retention amount under Purchase Order (which is deducted previously from invoices
entry.content: i got the same question. i can't find the documentation how to release the retainer. this help page seems to suggest to create a credit memo once the supplier invoices the retainer. but it doesn't say how https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c5b83a5abcff4efcb67a702ad5c71720/9cae7a1bf4834d91bfe9c659208caef9.html?locale=en-US&version=2021.002

entry.title: How to request the acquire data connectivity?
entry.content:
2 different connections are required on s4 and sac to have planning.

How to request the acquire data connectivity?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to request the acquire data connectivity?
entry.content:
To be able to use SAC planning features they need to configure the Import data connection. 

there are two ways to do that. 

  1. Import to S4/hana via Odata 
  2. Import to S4/hana via RFC 

For the RFC option, you will need to install and configure the Apache Tomcat, Analytics Agent and Jco. For that, they need OS access to the server where the SAP Cloud Connector is installed.

entry.title: Re: fb02-Assignment field (BSEG-ZUONR) field length will...
entry.content: I don't have time for this, I'm a beginner ABAP programmer. Any piece of code would help me more. thanks

entry.title: Re: Metering of SAP BTP GenAI offerings
entry.content:
Hi @Ivan-Mirisola,

Thank you for you response. I am able to add the GenAI hub services in the estimator.

My question is more specifically towards the wording stated in the standard documentation where it states that 1 token from GenAI hub is equal to 1000 tokens from LLM. So am I right in interpreting that if we purchase 1 token (input or output for any model) of GenAI hub on BTP, that will actually give us 1000 actual tokens from LLM (e.g. Azure Open AI service)?

If this understanding is correct then it becomes a very important selling point for GenAI hub on BTP rather than going with the other LLM providers.

Regards,
Saurabh

entry.title: Re: Remove Personalization aspect from SmartEdit
entry.content:
Hello! In order to resolve the pop-up errors involving the personalizationwebservices, it is recommended to install this extension even if you are not using. In addition, be aware that a regression error has affected the SAP Commerce Cloud 2211.17 involving this extension, requiring to upgrade the patch version to 18 where the fix was introduced.

Kind Regards,
Wesley
SAP Commerce Cloud - Product Support

entry.title: Re: Are there any best practice guidelines for client-spe...
entry.content: There are no restrictions in the enterprise management layer. You can configure the system like any system on premise or in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Instead of starting configuration from scratch or from client 000, you build on top of available EML content. Also see Enterprise Management Layer for SAP S/4HANA – Information Hub | SAP Blogs

entry.title: Public Cloud - Product Pictures on Sales Quotation Output Form
entry.content:
Dear Community,

we have the requirement that in the Sales Quotation Output Form not only the standard fields should be displayed but a product picture as well.

Our current struggle: There is no binding in the standard data binding to include product images. When looking at the data source extension there is also no way to include this.

Has anyone experience with this requirement and can exist or has an idea and can share this with me?

Thank you so much in advance.

entry.title: How to fix X-axis position in bar/column chart? Sap Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a very simple problem ( or at least I think so ) that I couldn't solve by myself.

I want to know if it is possible to fix the X-axis position in a bar/column chart for both negative and positive value.

This is the problem: 

        - If the bar/column chart displays a negative value, the X-axis is at the top of the chart

 

 

        - If the bar/column chart displays a positive value, the X-axis is at the bottom of the chart

 

I would like to have a chart where the X-axis is blocked in a fixed position, and then if the value displayed is positive, it is showed above the X-Axis; if the value displayed is negative, it is showed under the X-axis.

I have tried to lock the axis line as in the image below but it doesn't work

 

 

Thanks in advance for helping me.

entry.title: Re: Release Navigator for SAP Business Technology Platform by SAP Enterprise Support

Check it out now.

entry.title: Re: Electronic Invoicing in Malaysia
entry.content:
@TehKH - please see below:

1) Yes, the solution will be available to both S/4 and ECC. For details please check the link to the Regulatory Change Manager above. On-premise unless stated otherwise denotes ECC.

2) See point 1)

3) SAP DRC is comprised of two former solutions - Advanced Compliance Reporting (ACR) and Document Compliance, formerly known as DC or eDocument(s). DRC came into effect in October 2021. So the E-Invoicing portio of DRC is former eDocument, so to speak.

Ankur Goyal

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Is multi-level tier Advanced Intercompany stock transfer process also supported where the intermediate company would be there between shipping and receiving companies? I tried and could not find any documentation.

entry.title: Re: Item Price from last two AP Invoice
entry.content:
Hello Samudra, generally stock is valuated either at moving average price or standard price - so by this way it is not possible.

You can follow sales order specific valuation where purchased item is tagged to specific sales order & cost will be booked accordingly.

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Re: Asset Management as one of the main topics at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference
entry.content: Metro Map diagram is also a nice description, others refer to it as "The Snake". We however do not have this for the whole utility industry.

entry.title: Condition Contract - Transfer of settlement documents to COPA
entry.content:
Good morning,

I have reviewed SAP Note 2997203 - Transfer of WBRK settlement documents to COPA, and I have also attempted to execute program RWLF2063 to transfer an item to COPA, also tried the RWLF2065. However, the program does not execute. Upon debugging, I observed that the process stops at the function COPA_DOCUMENT_CHECK_START, specifically at the following point:

 

 
LOOP AT t_accit TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS
          WHERE awtyp EQ <row_acchd>-awtyp
            AND awref EQ <row_acchd>-awref
            AND aworg EQ <row_acchd>-aworg
*            AND NOT paobjnr IS INITIAL.
            AND paobjnr <> if_fco_copa_paobjnr=>c_init 
            AND paobjnr <> if_fco_copa_paobjnr=>c_zero.
          EXIT.
        ENDLOOP.

This occurs because the PAOBJNR field is initial. The error message received is "Document with unauthorized business transaction 'SD00'", KE/AD - 604. In the configuration, it is set to go to COPA in document 0S12, under Settlement Management, Settlement Documents, and Settlement Document Types. I have set Profitability Analysis as "Active apart from.." and also tried with "Active", but encountered the same error. Additionally, in pricing A10005, I have included condition Y005, which is the same as used in billing documents, and set it as statistical, as per KE4I settings, but it still did not work.

Anyone has this scenario using condition contract + COPA? How did you manage to transfer the documents?

entry.title: SAP MDK 23 UpdateEntity Error "A query for link targets returned zero targets"
entry.content:
Hi All!

We're migrating our MDK app from version 6.3.2 to 2310 and one of the issues we're facing is an error when triggering an UpdateEntity Action, which show us the following error:

 

mdk.trace.action: Error running action propagating error Action.Type.ODataService.UpdateEntity.1: Error: A query for link targets returned zero targets : Error: A query for link targets returned zero targets
    at ODataLinkUpdater.acquireTargets (file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:145676:19)
    at ODataLinkUpdater.execute (file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:145655:14)
    at ODataLinkUpdater.execute (file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:145537:19)
    at ODataUpdater.executeLinkUpdaters (file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:145302:33)
    at ODataUpdater.executeLinkers (file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:145287:14)
    at file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:145261:18
    at new Promise (<anonymous>)
    at ODataUpdater.execute (file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:145257:16)
    at ODataServiceProvider.updateEntity (file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:142259:33)
    at file:///data/data/com.vale.ptsdigitalqas/files/app/vendor.js:141341:34

 

 While debugging the app I tried to find some rule script which could be failing but this comes directly from the oData Update process.

The action is:

{
	"_Type": "Action.Type.ODataService.UpdateEntity",
	"ActionResult": {
		"_Name": "update"
	},
	"OnSuccess": "/PTSDigital/Actions/Logs/ExpiredAfterTwentyFourHours.action",
	"ShowActivityIndicator": true,
	"ActivityIndicatorText": "$(L,'saving')",
	"Target": {
		"Service": "/PTSDigital/Services/ptsdig.service",
		"EntitySet": "SafeWorkPermissions",
		"QueryOptions": "/PTSDigital/Rules/ClientData/ReturnSwpIdFromClientData.js"
	},
	"Properties": {
		"status_id": "EXPIRED",
		"status_language": "PT"
	},
	"UpdateLinks": [
		{
			"Property": "status",
			"Target": {
				"EntitySet": "Status",
				"QueryOptions": "$filter=id eq 'EXPIRED' and language eq 'PT'"
			},
			"_Enabled": true
		}
	]
}

 ...and I'm sure the rule in QueryOptions returned the expected value.

What I'm missing here?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Urgently !! Payable invoice and its Contracts?
entry.content:
Hello you can use below tables to get this info.

MIRO invoices - RBKP, RSEG (you will get PO number in this) then use EBKE table to get details of all documents related to PO.

If you also need details on open invoices (yet to be paid) use BSIK (vendor open item)/BKPF (Accounting document header) table in these you will get MIRO invoice number as well.

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Is it possible to include history of inactive users in "raw all lessons report" or a similar report?
entry.content:
I am looking for analytics that provide historical content completion data, even after users are inactive. We need to prove that users have accessed and/or completed content in the past, regardless of user or employment status.

It was great to get the information to access the "raw all lesson report" in another thread, but unfortunately it does not contain data for inactive users as far as I can see. 

Your insight is appreciated. 

Leticia Urrutia, Pacific Gas & Electric

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services and SAP Information Steward, as of November 2023
entry.content: At least you SAP guys , when posting, could use a more professional-looking handle... to make the content more credible.

entry.title: Error: To create a mapping, use the development system (3SL) or Q system (2SL).
entry.content:
Hi,

I´m triying to use the App Renumber G/L Accounts but i get this message:To create a mapping, use thSAP HANA Cloud, SAP Adaptive e development system (3SL) or Q system (2SL).

I have SAP S/4Hana Cloud 2402, i saw the note SAP 3289450 - Error ' To create a mapping, use the development system (3SL) or Q system (2SL) in 'Renumber G/L Accounts' but the Resolution says:System working as Designed

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK 23 UpdateEntity Error "A query for link targe...
entry.content: From the error, I am guessing that the query on the status entity filtering for id eq 'EXPIRED' and language eq 'PT' is not returning any results.  I would double check that first to ensure it is returning the expected data.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK OnValueChange event issue
entry.content: What version of MDK are you using?

entry.title: Re: Attribute layout in RTF report
entry.content: Hi!

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales in SAP S/4HANA OP/Private Cloud

Thanks,

Ankur Goyal

entry.title: Re: VA02 from an ABAP program
entry.content: @SantoshB you cannot directly update the Z field in the BAPI because it's a Z table. You would need to rely on any logic in user exit/BADIs to perform an update of a Z field in a Z table during order processing.

entry.title: Transfer order UB - Missing client but it does exist
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm facing a problem when I make a transfer order with conditions (S4 HANA CLOUD PUBLIC). Everything in the configuration is set and I proceed with the proces

In customizing I create the transfer order (app ME21N) and I'm able to finish the process. But when I try in the QM tenant (test) with the same client's ID I get these errors:

 

Not possible to determine shipping data for material XXX

Please maintain sales org./division/distr. channel in supplying plant XXX

Customer  does not exist (please change entry in plant XXX)

 

The customer master and the material master are OK, they have all the information. I think the error is because theres no transport order for the activity 101926 Define Shipping Data for Plants. In this activity I set the customer 9 and this customer is in both systems, with the exact same information.

 

What can I do to solve this problem?

 

Thank you!

entry.title: Resource Schema Configuration in SAP Build Apps Destination to BPA Service
entry.content:
I have a BPA project that I want to use an API Unattended Trigger to execute.  I am creating a Destination to the BPA service instance and when I get to the Resource schema, the information in tutorials is a bit confusing.

What fields should be going into the schema?  Is it the values I want to pass to the BAP process or would those go down in "Additional Inputs?  Is there something else I need to add in the Resource schema?

My end goal is to have a Build Apps application execute the trigger, passing in the parameters it has gathered.

Any ideas and/or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks!

Ron

entry.title: Re: Attribute layout in RTF report
entry.content:
Hi!

I think I'm doing things correctly... 😐

First, I added a 2 column table to my RTF template. (see step1.png)

Then I generated my report. The attributes that I want IN my table were lower than my table. So, I returned in my RTF template and lowered the table. I regenerated my report and my attributes did go even lower. (see step2.png) My conclusion is that the report content appends to the RTF template content.

What am I doing wrong?

Maybe I would need a step-by-step procedure, please.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Wrong image in repeated containers (SEVERAL BUG)
entry.content:
More info about this bug. Anyone here can suggest a workaround?

This is the configuration of the background of the container.

This exact config is working in production to the same API, using the same URLs.

I am waiting for this to release a new version, that is critical ASAP.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: deploying email sending project in outlook automated...
entry.content: no, I do not have attributes in the agent

entry.title: Re: Enhancements in FSCM Dispute Management
entry.content:
@Bohdan 

I have a question about changing the user who created an active Case ID but the user is no longer in SAP.     (I am in security and we have audit restrictions of recreating terminated users in PRD).

It looks like we could utilize the "SET_SINGLE_ATTRIBUTE_VALUE" method to create a customized update process to change the value of the "processor" (i guess is would be) from the old value to a new value and potentially do it off of the Case GUID which is unique.

I was going to pitch this idea to my development team but wanted to get your thoughts on this as a way to correct the dreaded "UDM_DISPUTE Error - Entry XXXXX does not exist in USR02"

If not can you think of another solution besides adding back the ID in the SYSTEM?

From above:

There is also a setter method that allows you to change the value of case attributes. You can use the method SET_SINGLE_ATTRIBUTE_VALUE to change the value of the case attribute. Source code below shows how to update the value of the case title:

try.
  im_case->set_single_attribute_value(
    im_srmadid = im_case->case_title
    im_value   = 'Case Title: New Value' ).
  catch cx_srm_framework .
  catch cx_scmg_case_attribute .
endtry.


Big advantage of using this BAdI is that you can update many different attributes within the same enhancement. FSCM module provides a lot of different BAdIs that can be used to update only one specific attribute (e.g., case priority, external references, processor etc.).

entry.title: Re: SAP DM error : ERROR_USER_IS_NOT_AUTHORIZED_IN_CLOUD_...
entry.content:
"Unable to generate authentication token for ..." looks like there is something wrong with the logon settings on application side. (The application being "SAP DM" in this case.) The "high level" view of how this works is as follows:

  1. An end user logs on to the Cloud application (SAP DM), usually via HTTPS.
  2. The application then forwards a SAML or JWT token for that user to the Cloud Connector (SCC).
  3. The SCC converts this token to an X.509 certificate and uses that for logon to the Backend System.

The last step is the one that's failing -- either because the token is missing or because its invalid. (Unfortunately the trace is too short to see the underlying reason.)

Note: the users defined in the user configuration tab have nothing to do with this: they are used only for administrating the cloud account and the technical connections between SCC and the Cloud VM, but not for login on to backend systems!

So I would suggest:

  1. Verify whether during the DM upgrade anything has changed in the logon configuration of the DM app.
  2. If you can't find anything, temporarily set the "SCC Loggers" to highest level, reproduce the error once, and attach the last 2-3 pages of the ljs_trace.log. (Best in text form, not as screenshot.)
    Note: do not increase the "Other Loggers". These only create lots of trace output that is not needed for this problem.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM error : ERROR_USER_IS_NOT_AUTHORIZED_IN_CLOUD_CONNECTOR
entry.content: PS: adding the Connectivity Service tag. They will have more knowhow about how this works on cloud side.

entry.title: Re: Communication Arrangement Check Connection Failed
entry.content:
Hi, Thanks for taking time and helping me resolve the issue.

In the tutorial that i mentioned. It does not talk about creating the Destination in SAP BTP. I showed that screen shot to mention that the Target Host name that i used Communication Systems--> RFC Settings --> Target Host is the same as the value(Host name) that was used in creating the Destination.

entry.title: Onboarding Custom Task
entry.content: In the 1H upgrade, I believe there will be the ability to create an Onboarding custom task and add attachments.  Will this custom task be available to candidates that are in the pipeline and completing their new hire paperwork.  In other words, the candidate is not an employee yet.  Can the custom task be presented like the new hire tiles on the homepage to the candidate?

entry.title: Re: Statistical Sales Condition in Margin Analysis
entry.content:
@Raman_Rana 
When the customer makes the payment within the Payment terms days, the customer gets the actual cash-discount. This actual cash discount is posted to the Leading Ledger during the customer Payment.

Thus, the previously posted cash-discount (statistical condition based) in Extension ledger should be knocked-off/reversed. How does this reversal get handled in Ext. Ledger?

entry.title: SuccessFactors Recruiting Posting Subject Matter Expert Office Hours (French/English)
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the ‘Will you be attending?’ option will NOT register you for the event.

To register for this event, please click here: Registration details can be found with our full schedule here (Authentication Required)

Please join us for our Recruiting Posting Subject Matter Expert office hours!

If you have questions about your SuccessFactors Recruiting Posting product regarding best practices, use cases, 'how to' questions, etc. then please join our call and we'll have our expert answer your question live.

These sessions are held in an open online forum with other customers participating. There is no pre-planned content or presentation. Time is spent answering the questions you bring to the call. No advance registration required.


Note: Attendees can use the in-system audio and have the system call you directly or attendees can call in manually. To locate the call-in number for your region along with the passcode, please click on the information dropdown in the upper right corner of the meeting (indicated by an italicized i in a circle icon).

entry.title: Removing Translate Manually for Standard recordings
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a requirement to translate the O&N standard recordings to French using Machine Translation. However, all explanation and action bubbles (and there are many!) are set to Translate Manually, meaning I need to manually remove each of them. Does anyone know of a way whether through a function in EN or through a script that will remove the Translate Manually option for the bubbles in both the Demo and Practice modes?

TIA

entry.title: Re: How to create a sequence number in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Samrat,

If I'm not mistaking you are referring to this Informatica Sequence Generator transformation

My first thought while creating Data Flows you would create a calculated columns with Projection operator and using some functions available, check out Function Reference for Data Transformation Language  But unfortunately there is no sequence generator like functions among --seems to be 84 in total-- functions.

There are two options left:

  1. You will use Script operator and generate number with Python script in still Data Flow > Projection > Calculated Column. Check out Script Operator Python Reference
  2. Create SQL View and you would use ROW_NUMBER Window function.

@XaviPolo answered one similar question here Add a Column with progressive number in Dataflow (Datasphere)

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Is there a way to create variants for the job that releases changes from the Maint to Prod branch
entry.content:
I am trying to find a way to control the Release changes in Solution Documentation through a batch job with variants to select the different options rather than manually running it through SOLDOC.  Has anyone encountered this kind of scenario?

Thanks in advance.

Burak

entry.title: Deployed Business Process Automation not showing in Monitoring Processes and Workflows
entry.content:
I created a new Process Automation project from scratch with only an Automation in it.  I released and deployed it to my Public environment but when I go into Build -> Monitoring -> Manage -> Processes and Workflows, it's not there.  I tested it in Editable mode and it works just fine.

I'm sure this is something silly I've done but I can't figure out what it is.

Thanks in advance for any and all help.  🙂

Ron

entry.title: SAP Community Subscriptions and E-mail Notifications
entry.content:
In January 2024, SAP updated the entire SAP Community platform, and the emails of new publications, questions and articles that you were subscribed to and received via email SAP Central Communication Service notification@sap.com (Example in the image below: "New blog post tagged: SAP Sales Cloud"), is no longer sent.

In this blog, I will demonstrate how you subscribe to continue receiving community emails

When logging in with your P-user on the SAP Community platform community.sap.com, in the right corner of the screen click on the person's icon > “My Content”.

Then, on your profile, look for the option “My Settings”.

In “Subscriptions and Notifications” you can configure how you want to be notified and what you want to subscribe to.

For example, at the end of a community post, you will find “SAP Managed tags”, click on the tags you want to track and be notified by email.

Just click on “Subscribe”.

In “Subscriptions & Notifications” > “My Subscriptions”, you can list which tags you are subscribed to.

In “Subscriptions & Notifications” > “Notification Settings”, it is important to review your Email Notifications to ensure that you will receive the email.

In the image below, an example of the new format for receiving community notifications.

I hope this blog has helped you subscribe to content that interests you from the SAP community. If you found this blog useful, please like, follow, and share the knowledge.

Feel free to leave your comment, or ask any questions related to the topic.

Clovis Müller

entry.title: SAP CLOUD ALM - Impact of Closing a Defect on the Test Case Execution?
entry.content:
Not being fully versed yet in how Defects in Cloud ALM work yet, I had a customer ask me "What happens to the Test Execution and Test Step once a Defect has been resolved?" Best I can tell, if a Defect is in any other status than Closed, there will be a small Alert Icon (n) in the Test Step where the Defect was detected. See below. However, once the Defect is Closed, the Alert Icon (n) is no longer present. The link to the Defect also appears to be removed. 

While I am able to see Team, Responsible, Planned Completion Date, etc. via the Defects App, without drilling into each Defect, we cannot see which Test Case it was assigned originally. Are there plans to allow reporting of Defect status by Test Case? I have checked both Test Execution Analysis and Defect Reporting via Analytics and have not seen a way to report on Defects by Test Case. 

Thank you for the help!

entry.title: SAP CPQ - The learning Series
entry.content:
SAP CPQ blogs: Learning Series


Topic: Integrating SAP CPQ with SAP ECC, SAP CRM, and Salesforce Introduction:
SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) software revolutionizes the sales process by streamlining configuration, pricing, and quoting tasks. Integrating SAP CPQ with SAP ECC, SAP CRM, and Salesforce extends its capabilities and enhances overall business efficiency.


Integration with SAP ECC:

1. Seamless Data Flow:
- SAP CPQ integrates seamlessly with SAP ECC, ensuring a smooth flow of data between systems.
- Real-time synchronization of product data, pricing information, and customer details eliminates data discrepancies and enhances accuracy.

2. Unified Sales Process:
- Integration with SAP ECC enables a unified sales process by connecting sales activities with backend operations.
- Sales representatives can access up-to-date product information, inventory levels, and order status directly from SAP ECC within the CPQ interface.

3. Order Management:
- Orders generated through SAP CPQ are automatically transferred to SAP ECC for order processing and fulfillment.
- This integration streamlines order management processes, reducing manual intervention and minimizing errors.

4. Financial Integration:
- SAP CPQ integration with SAP ECC facilitates financial reconciliation by syncing pricing and billing information.
- Finance teams have visibility into sales transactions, enabling accurate revenue recognition and financial reporting.

Integration with SAP CRM:

1. 360-Degree Customer View:
- Integrating SAP CPQ with SAP CRM provides sales teams with a comprehensive view of customer interactions and preferences.
- Sales representatives can leverage CRM data to personalize quotes and offer tailored solutions to customers.

2. Opportunity Management:
- SAP CPQ seamlessly integrates with SAP CRM's opportunity management module, enabling sales reps to create quotes directly from opportunities.
- This integration accelerates the quote-to-order process, leading to faster deal closures and increased revenue.

3. Lead Conversion:
- Leads captured in SAP CRM can be converted into quotes in SAP CPQ with a single click, streamlining lead-to-order conversion.
- Sales teams can capitalize on lead opportunities more effectively, enhancing overall sales performance.

4. Reporting and Analytics:
- Integration between SAP CPQ and SAP CRM enables unified reporting and analytics across sales and quoting activities.
- Sales managers gain insights into quote conversion rates, sales pipeline health, and sales team performance, empowering data-driven decision-making.

Integration with Salesforce:

1. Bi-Directional Data Sync:
- SAP CPQ integrates seamlessly with Salesforce, facilitating bi-directional data synchronization between the two platforms.
- Product configurations, pricing updates, and quote information are automatically synced between SAP CPQ and Salesforce, ensuring data consistency.

2. Quote Generation from Salesforce:
- Sales representatives can generate quotes directly within Salesforce using SAP CPQ integration.
- By leveraging Salesforce's familiar interface, sales teams can create and manage quotes more efficiently, enhancing productivity.

3. Workflow Automation:
- Integration with Salesforce enables workflow automation, allowing businesses to define custom approval processes for quotes.
- Sales managers can set up automated notifications and escalations, ensuring timely follow-ups and approvals.

4. Enhanced Collaboration:
- SAP CPQ integration with Salesforce promotes collaboration between sales and other departments.
- Sales reps can collaborate with marketing, finance, and product teams within the Salesforce environment, streamlining communication and decision-making.

Conclusion:
Integrating SAP CPQ with SAP ECC, SAP CRM, and Salesforce empowers businesses to optimize their sales processes, improve customer engagement, and drive revenue growth. By leveraging the combined capabilities of these platforms, organizations can achieve greater efficiency, accuracy, and competitiveness in today's dynamic market landscape.

This comprehensive integration enables businesses to harness the power of SAP CPQ within their existing ERP and CRM ecosystems, maximizing the value of their technology investments and delivering exceptional customer experiences.

Topic 2: SAP CPQ's quote generation process:

Introduction to SAP CPQ Quote Generation:
SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) software empowers businesses to generate accurate and professional quotes efficiently. Quote generation in SAP CPQ involves a series of steps that streamline the process from product configuration to pricing and quotation delivery.

Configuration Phase:
1. Product Selection:
- Users begin by selecting the products or services they wish to include in the quote.
- SAP CPQ offers a user-friendly interface for product selection, allowing users to browse through available options or search for specific items.

2. Dynamic Configuration:
- SAP CPQ supports dynamic product configuration, enabling users to customize products based on their requirements.
- Users can select product features, options, and accessories to create personalized configurations tailored to their needs.

3. Rules and Constraints:
- SAP CPQ enforces business rules and constraints during the configuration process to ensure the validity of configurations.
- Rules govern compatibility between product components, pricing restrictions, and other constraints, preventing users from creating invalid configurations.

4. Visual Configuration:
- Visual configuration tools in SAP CPQ provide users with a graphical representation of product configurations.
- Users can visualize product variations and make informed decisions about their selections, enhancing the overall user experience.

Pricing Phase:
1. Price Calculation:
- Once the product configuration is finalized, SAP CPQ calculates the price based on predefined pricing rules and conditions.
- Pricing rules consider factors such as product features, quantity discounts, customer-specific pricing, and contractual agreements.

2. Discounting and Promotions:
- SAP CPQ supports discounting and promotional pricing to incentivize customers and drive sales.
- Users can apply discounts based on volume, customer loyalty, or promotional campaigns, with the flexibility to set expiration dates and eligibility criteria.

3. Real-Time Pricing:
- SAP CPQ integrates with backend systems such as SAP ECC or Salesforce to retrieve real-time pricing information.
- This ensures that quotes reflect the most current pricing data, reducing discrepancies and improving accuracy.

4. Margin Analysis:
- SAP CPQ provides margin analysis tools to evaluate the profitability of quotes.
- Users can assess the impact of pricing adjustments on margins and make informed decisions to maximize profitability.

Quotation Delivery Phase:
1. Document Generation:
- SAP CPQ generates professional-looking quote documents based on predefined templates.
- Users can customize quote templates with company branding, logos, and legal disclaimers to maintain brand consistency.

2. Quote Approval Workflow:
- SAP CPQ supports customizable approval workflows for quotes, ensuring compliance with internal policies and procedures.
- Quotes can be routed to designated approvers based on predefined criteria, with notifications and escalations to facilitate timely approvals.

3. Electronic Signature Integration:
- SAP CPQ integrates with electronic signature platforms such as DocuSign or Adobe Sign for seamless quote approval.
- Users can electronically sign quotes directly within the SAP CPQ interface, eliminating the need for manual signatures and expediting the approval process.

4. Delivery Channels:
- SAP CPQ offers multiple delivery channels for quotes, including email, print, and online portals.
- Users can choose the most appropriate delivery method based on customer preferences and business requirements.

Conclusion:
SAP CPQ's quote generation capabilities enable businesses to streamline the quoting process, improve sales efficiency, and enhance customer satisfaction. By automating configuration, pricing, and quotation delivery, SAP CPQ empowers sales teams to generate accurate quotes quickly, ultimately driving revenue growth and profitability.

Topic 3: SAP CPQ Approval process
Introduction to SAP CPQ Approval Workflow:
SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) software provides a robust approval workflow engine that streamlines the process of quote approval. The approval workflow in SAP CPQ allows businesses to define custom approval processes, route quotes to designated approvers, and track approval status in real-time.

Setting Up Approval Workflow:
1. Workflow Configuration:
- Administrators can configure approval workflows in SAP CPQ using an intuitive interface.
- Workflow parameters such as approval stages, approver roles, and approval conditions are defined based on business requirements.

2. Approval Rules:
- SAP CPQ supports the creation of flexible approval rules that dictate when quotes require approval.
- Rules can be based on factors such as quote value, customer type, discount amount, or specific product configurations.

3. Multi-Level Approval:
- SAP CPQ facilitates multi-level approval hierarchies, allowing quotes to be routed to multiple approvers sequentially or in parallel.
- Administrators can define escalation paths and fallback options to ensure timely resolution of approval requests.

4. Conditional Approval:
- Conditional approval rules in SAP CPQ enable dynamic routing of quotes based on specific criteria.
- Quotes that meet predefined conditions are automatically routed to designated approvers, streamlining the approval process.

Approval Process Execution:
1. Quote Submission:
- Sales representatives submit quotes for approval within the SAP CPQ interface.
- Quotes are automatically assigned to the appropriate approval workflow based on predefined rules and conditions.

2. Approval Routing:
- SAP CPQ routes quotes to designated approvers based on the configured approval workflow.
- Approvers receive notifications and task assignments within the system, prompting them to review and approve quotes.

3. Review and Approval:
- Approvers can review quote details, including product configurations, pricing information, and discount levels, within the SAP CPQ interface.
- Approvals can be granted, rejected, or deferred with comments, providing transparency and accountability throughout the process.

4. Escalation and Notification:
- SAP CPQ triggers automated notifications and escalations for pending approval tasks.
- If an approver fails to take action within a specified timeframe, the workflow escalates the approval request to designated backup approvers or managers.

Monitoring and Reporting:
1. Real-Time Tracking:
- SAP CPQ provides real-time visibility into the status of approval workflows.
- Users can track the progress of approval requests, view pending tasks, and monitor approval cycle times to identify bottlenecks and optimize efficiency.

2. Audit Trail:
- SAP CPQ maintains a comprehensive audit trail of all approval activities, including approvals, rejections, comments, and escalations.
- Administrators can review the audit trail to ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.

3. Performance Metrics:
- SAP CPQ offers built-in reporting and analytics tools to measure approval workflow performance.
- Key metrics such as approval cycle time, approval rates, and approval bottlenecks are available for analysis and optimization.

4. Continuous Improvement:
- Based on insights gained from monitoring and reporting, businesses can continuously refine and optimize their approval workflows.
- SAP CPQ allows for iterative improvements to approval rules, routing logic, and notification settings to enhance overall efficiency and effectiveness.

Conclusion:
SAP CPQ's approval workflow capabilities enable businesses to streamline the quote approval process, improve operational efficiency, and ensure compliance with internal policies and procedures. By providing a flexible and configurable approval engine, SAP CPQ empowers organizations to implement custom approval workflows tailored to their unique business requirements, ultimately driving faster quote approvals and accelerating sales cycles.

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 1
entry.content:
{
  "specversion": "1.0",
  "type": "com.example.myapp.resource.created",
  "source": "/myapp/resources/1234567890",
  "id": "A234-1234-1234",
  "time": "2023-03-05T18:00:00Z",
  "datacontenttype": "application/json",
  "data": {
    "resourceId": "1234567890",
    "resourceName": "My Resource",
    "resourceType": "myapp.com/resources"
  }
}

entry.title: Leveraging Backoffice Roles: Effective Restriction of Backoffice Access in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
SAP Commerce Cloud's Backoffice accessibility control differs from the conventional access management implemented in the platform module. In this blog post, I'll delve into how to restrict access to a widget in Backoffice using a custom Backoffice role, offering a simple and effective example.

To start, let’s look at the fundamentals of a Backoffice Role:

  1. It is implemented as a subtype of UserGroup.
  2. It carries an 'authorities' attribute, a list of String, with each representing a unique authority right. This is why it is also called the “Authority Group”.

To limit access to any widget instance in Backoffice, the following steps can be taken:

  1. In any widget instance, select the 'access' attribute to specify an predefined or custom authority right.
  2. Create a Backoffice Role using a list of authorities that includes the one specified above.
  3. Assign the Backoffice Role to an existing or custom user so that they can gain access to the assigned authority right.
  4. When the user logs into Backoffice, they will be able to access the specified widget.

Let's unpack these steps further using a simple example, illustrating the method of restricting access to a custom-made Backoffice cockpit with an individual Backoffice role.

1. Restrict a Custom Cockpit with 'access'

For starters, I need to prepare a custom Backoffice cockpit. I can log into Backoffice as admin and enter the Application Orchestrator (AO) mode by pressing F4. In the Backoffice Perspective Container widget, I can add a new a new cockpit using the BorderLayout Widget:

After the new cockpit is created, I'll edit it and name it “demoCockpit”.

Upon exiting AO mode, I'll find the newly created cockpit visible on the interface.

Now i'll impose access restrictions on "demoCockpit", simply return to AO mode and edit the settings. I can find an 'access' tab, into which I can enter the term "viewDemoCockpit" as the authority right. This grants Backoffice users with "viewDemoCockpit" authority right the ability to view "demoCockpit".

Please note that if multiple authority rights are specified in the field, it indicates an "any" situation. This means that a user with any one of the listed authority rights can access this cockpit.

Now exit the AO mode again, and I can notice that the demoCockpit is no longer visible, even to the admin user.

Actually, for better practices, instead of doing all the steps described above, I can create a new Backoffice extension with the yBackoffice template and simply add a few lines of code to <mybackoffice>-backoffice-widgets.xml:

 

<widget-extension widgetId="backofficeMainSlot">
	<widget id="demoCockpit"
		widgetDefinitionId="com.hybris.cockpitng.borderlayout"
		slotId="perspectives" title="demo Cockpit" template="false"
		access="viewDemoCockpit">
	</widget>
</widget-extension>

 

Then I'll rebuild my Commerce Cloud system including the newly created extension, and do a reset on the widgets.xml in AO mode to get my setting above loaded.

2. Create a Backoffice Role

The next step involves creating a new Backoffice role with the specified authority right. I can easily navigate to User | user groups to create a new Backoffice Role:

I'll name it "demoBackofficeRole" and hit "Finish" to create it:

I'll find the newly created Backoffice role and edit it, then switch to the administration tab to find the "Authorities" attribute:

I'll add a new authority right with the value "viewDemoCockpit", which was specified before in the custom demoCockpit widget:

Certainly, I can add more authority rights so that users with the Backoffice Role have complete access to all the authority rights mentioned in the "Authorities" list.

3. Assign a User to the Backoffice Role and Verify the Backoffice Access Restriction

Now, I can create a new user, e.g. under User | Employees:

I'll type “testUser” as the ID/name, and assign the user to the "demoBackofficeRole".

With the Backoffice Role assignment, the user is granted the right "viewDemoCockpit" specified before. Don't forget to set a password to the user and enable Backoffice login (in the administration tab of the user).

Let's verify everything together. I'll log out Backoffice as admin and log back in as the testUser. There you go, I can see the demoCockpit now, even if admin can't see it.

Perhaps you have also noticed that the testUser can see other cockpits in the Backoffice. What if you want to restrict the view for the testUser? No problem! You can control the context of the "perspective-chooser" component in order to limit the visibility of cockpits for all users with the authority of "viewDemoCockpit":

<context component="perspective-chooser">
	<y:perspective-chooser xmlns:y="http://www.hybris.com/cockpitng/config/perspectiveChooser">
		<y:authority name="viewDemoCockpit">
			<y:perspective id="demoCockpit"/>
		</y:authority>
	</y:perspective-chooser>
</context>

I can open the <mybackoffice>-backoffice-config.xml file and insert the above code. Then I'll log into the Backoffice as admin again, enter the AO mode and perform a reset on the "cockpit-config.xml" file, such that the changes will take effect and the testUser with the "viewDemoCockpit" authority will only be able to see the "demoCockpit" in Backoffice.

By following these straightforward steps, I can restrict the access of any widgets in Backoffice using custom Backoffice roles. While I've demonstrated a basic understanding of this process, you can delve deeper into advanced features with the help portal documentations: Business Roles in Backoffice.

As an important alternative, you could also consider enlisting in our expert deep dive live session on "Introducing Backoffice Access Management in SAP Commerce Cloud" to get live demos and comprehensive explanations from our team.

Here's looking forward to our next live sessions on optimal usage of the SAP Commerce Cloud platform. Remember, there are other Expert Deep Dive Live Sessions to help you unlock the full potential of SAP Commerce Cloud! (e.g., Integration API module, Dynatrace, Composable Storefront, Scripting Support, Hot Folders, Interceptors, etc.) See you soon!

entry.title: Visual Studio failing while adding the deployment configuration of the SAPUI5 application
entry.content: Visual Studio Code is throwing an error while I am trying to add the deployment configuration of the SAPUI5 application. The error message is "TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'www-authenticate') To continue with project generation choose a different deployment target or skip adding deploy config." Any guidance on fixing this issue is highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: US Form I-9 Concepts for SuccessFactors Onboarding 2.0
entry.content: Hi.  How do our admins correct the hire date on the I-9 to update E-verify or do they need to contact the gov agency to make the corrections?

entry.title: Re: Include PO confirmation reference field in MIGO recei...
entry.content: This is finally done.  Instead of adding a new field to MIGO, the consultants have made a change which copies the data from this Reference field to display in the Text field of MIGO.   Turns out it was relatively simple.

entry.title: Scheduling an exam with CER006 SAP Certification Exam, Six Attempts
entry.content:
Hi!

I am trying to schedule an exam for E_ACTCLD_23 SAP Certified Specialist - SAP Activate for Cloud Solutions Project Manager.  #SAP Activate 

I have purchased "CER006 SAP Certification Exam, Six Attempts" for exam. However, when I click on shedule an exam, it shows up that I do not have any exam attempts. Could you help me fix this problem? Thanks

VR

entry.title: Hello dears i need this scope item to be localized at egypt event based production costin 3f0
Re: Solution Manager (without ChaRM) integration with Azu...
entry.content: Thanks vera, for sharing gCTS Note information, gCTS is also a consideration. CTS+ can be configured on Solution Manager so wanted to see if anyone has experienced it.

entry.title: oData.Create is giving unexpected token error for batch calls
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am working on creating purchasing contract and using standard service API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV. oData. create is giving error though it is creating a purchasecontract. I am getting a very unique error as "unexpected token(l)...location is not valid json". It is blocking me from proceeding further. 

While I am disabling batch call system is able to create contract without any error. Can someone help me urgently.

I am attaching the screenshot.

Your help will be highly appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: VSR Optimizer Cost and Constraints Calculations
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

We have been trying to find a way to create a condition for certain suppliers, we want their orders to be automatically planned as LTL or FTL depending on the volume, because we have some suppliers that sometimes ship less than 10 pallets, and sometimes more than the 10 pallets (which is the pallet limit for LTL shipments have maximun  10 pallets and 12,000 kg)

Thank u for your support 

Have a excellent day and sunny regards from Mexico.

entry.title: Re: Hierarchy with directory issue when sharing with othe...
entry.content:
Hello @PongsatornK,

There is one SAP KBA 3265465 - Facing Issues to Deploy a View that Uses Shared Objects as Sources suggesting that Ideally, shared objects should have quotes around them. You will need to add a double quote to the shared objects' names.

But I think it's for if you create / deploy SQL View. Probably it does not fit into your case.

Secondly you mentioned you shared the fact in ADMIN space. I think since the hierarchy is on Cost Center object you need to share Cost Center too with HR space, then you would be able to deploy the fact that you create in HR space.

Check you this reference information Sharing Tables and Views To Other Spaces 

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: How to Pass multiple fields(Non Key) in Odata query(URI)
entry.content:
Hi Odata Experts,

How can i pass multiple parameters (non-key fields along with a key field) in a Odata query(URI) in the Gateway WITHOUT defining underlying get_entitySET( ) method?

The requirment is that these non-key fields along with Key field are important to fetch data from underlying FM.

 

Thanks,

Ricky

entry.title: Re: 403 Forbidden error in CAP app when accessing from BTP
entry.content:
Hi , I am also facing the same issue . I have created and deployed an application From BAS to Cloud Foundry Space as a HTML 5 Application. The data from the Cloud Connector Destination is fetched from the BAS when i use the CURL command . Also, The preview of the application is displayed correctly . But when i deploy it to Cloud i am not able to get the data from the On Premises system using Cloud Connector. when I debugged the call in Google Chrome , I am getting a 403 Forbidden error and the reference Policy as Strict-Origin-Cross-Origin

Any help in resolving this issue would be appriciated

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now - Is there an automated data retention...
entry.content:
Hi Katy,

There is no functionality in Enable Now to automatically inform you about content that should be checked but you can run a report that allows you to export the details of all content objects in a Workarea, including the date of creation and the date of late modification.  You could then sort the content in a spreadsheet, by modification date, and determine what content to focus on.

To extract this detail into a 3rd party reporting tool, such as PowerBI, use the URL:

https://[customername].enable-now.cloud.sap/wa/[workareaID]/?{"out":["entity.type","entity.uid","entity.caption","entity.language","entity.hidden","entity.sub_type","entity.created_by","entity.creation_time","entity.modified_by","entity.modification_time","entity.categories","entity.content_categories","entity.keywords"]}

The text highlighted in red should be replaced with the details of your Enable Now system.

If you are not sure how to extract data from Enable Now manager using take a look at the last part of this video, that discussed the use of a third party report tool, such as PowerBI, to extract data from Manager.  Additionally, go through the content in Info Center on this topic, that explains everything that you need to know.

Regards
Shane
